acceptance_targets: [ch15-F001] PASS if each game segment in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:64-243 contains at least one clearly readable choice/consequence beat that is not just score movement and that directly alters the next rally approach; FAIL if any game remains only tactical exchange plus scoreboard updates.

[ch15-F002] PASS if the revised beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:454 includes explicit spoken wording and an immediate observable consequence in the car; FAIL if the prose still uses generic placeholders like "swore" without content.

[FBR-04] PASS if targeted lines no longer rely on the same 'landed' emotional verb or 'It was not... It was...' binary structure and each beat is conveyed through distinct concrete language; FAIL if the template pattern remains visibly repeated in the listed lines.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each game segment in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:64-243 contains at least one clearly readable choice/consequence beat that is not just score movement and that directly alters the next rally approach; FAIL if any game remains only tactical exchange plus scoreboard updates.
findings.problem: The quarterfinal sequence sustains tactical clarity, but the middle of the chapter over-allocates to rally score mechanics and under-allocates to non-score consequence turns (class pressure, agreement cost, and relationship leverage), so escalation reads as incremental rather than transformational.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:64-170 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:212-243, compress repetitive shot-by-shot runs and add one explicit non-score stake pivot per game (e.g., agreement enforcement pressure, social-class humiliation, father-daughter authority shift) that changes Anika's next tactical decision.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:454 includes explicit spoken wording and an immediate observable consequence in the car; FAIL if the prose still uses generic placeholders like "swore" without content.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: a high-pressure impact beat is rendered as euphemistic summary ("He swore under his breath") instead of specific language/event texture. This is exactly the scrubbed-profanity failure the constitution forbids, and it weakens scene force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:454 so the utterance is concrete (quoted wording) and socially embodied (who hears it, how Anika reacts, or how the crowded car registers it) rather than summarized as generic swearing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if targeted lines no longer rely on the same 'landed' emotional verb or 'It was not... It was...' binary structure and each beat is conveyed through distinct concrete language; FAIL if the template pattern remains visibly repeated in the listed lines.
findings.problem: Recurring prose-formula overuse creates mantra diction across chapters: repeated emotional-impact templates (notably 'X landed...' and binary 'It was not X. It was Y.') flatten line-level surprise and violate the constitution's anti-recurring-formula threshold.
findings.rewrite_direction: Line-edit the repeated templates at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2452, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2717, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3202, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4575, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5628, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7092, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1517, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4158, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5501, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6407 to use scene-specific action, syntax, and image rather than reusable templates.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
